vocabulary.meaning: The early part of the day
A plan of activities with times

phrases.example: Excuse me, what time is it?
I wake up early every day.

sentences.en: I usually wake up at seven in the morning.

sentences.bn: সে প্রতি সন্ধ্যায় রাতের খাবার রান্না করে।

sentences.en: I take a shower before breakfast.
sentences.bn: আমি সকালের নাস্তার আগে গোসল করি।

sentences.en: We go to bed at ten o'clock.

quiz.explanation: Routine means a set of regular activities you do every day.
With 'I', we use the base form: wake up.
'Good night' is said before sleeping or late in the evening.

quiz.q: 'I take a shower before breakfast' means:

story.title: Mira's Perfect Morning
story.body:
Every morning, Mira wakes up at 6 o'clock. First, she brushes her teeth and takes a quick shower.

Then she eats breakfast — usually eggs and toast.

She checks her schedule on her phone. Today she has an important meeting at 9 AM.

"I must not be late!" she says.

She picks up her bag and leaves home at 8 o'clock.

On the bus, she listens to English podcasts to practice.

Mira has a great routine. She always starts her day with energy and a smile!

comprehension.q: What time does Mira wake up?
What does Mira do on the bus?

listening.title: Morning Phone Call
listening.script:
Mom: "Rafi! Wake up! It's already seven o'clock!"

Rafi: "Five more minutes, Mom..."

Mom: "No! Breakfast is ready. You'll be late for school!"

Rafi: "Okay! I'm getting up now."

Mom: "Don't forget your schedule. You have math class first."

Rafi: "I know. I checked it last night."

Mom: "Come quickly before it gets cold!"

Rafi: "Coming! Good morning, Mom!"

Mom: "Good morning, dear. Now hurry up!"

comprehension.q: What time is it in the story?
What class does Rafi have first?

speaking.prompt: Talk about what you do every morning. Use: wake up, breakfast, and at least one time expression.
speaking.example: Every morning I wake up at 6 AM. I brush my teeth and take a shower. Then I eat breakfast and go to school.
tips: Use 'first', 'then', 'after that' to connect your sentences
Mention specific times like '6 AM', '7:30 AM'
